content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use WHATWG URL parsing and a documented equivalence policy without collapsing query, case, port, path, fragment, or percent-encoding distinctions unsafely.

hints: For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Url Identity model · Theory Feed Ts Url Identity, name the reader, publisher, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, tenant, URL, unknown XML, runtime validation, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, PostgreSQL, Express, handle, accessibility, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Feed Ts Url Identity precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Url Identity model · Theory Feed Ts Url Identity, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 1776 during an out-of-order Promise and AbortSignal rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile direct and autodiscovery, URL identity, redirects, SSRF, and provenance; browser TypeScript must remain deterministic and free of Node, network, database, and host effects; then change one PostgreSQL constraint, transaction, migration, upsert, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use WHATWG URL parsing and a documented equivalence policy without collapsing query, case, port, path, fragment, or percent-encoding distinctions unsafely.

For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Url Identity model · Theory Feed Ts Url Identity, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Calling URL.toString produces a universally canonical source identity.
